employee_eligibility_conditions.notes: The 12 months do not need to be consecutive. Service accrued more than 7 years ago shall not be counted. Military service under USERRA counts toward this requirement. 52 weeks of employment equals 12 months.
employee_eligibility_conditions.source_text: an employee must: (1) have worked for the State of Tennessee for at least twelve (12) months

employee_eligibility_conditions.notes: Includes overtime hours. Employees returning from USERRA-covered military service receive credit for hours that would have been worked during military service based on pre-service work schedule.
employee_eligibility_conditions.source_text: have worked at least one thousand, two-hundred and fifty (1,250) hours during the year preceding the start of the leave.

employee_eligibility_conditions.notes: Service accrued more than 7 years ago shall not be counted toward the 12-month minimum requirement.
employee_eligibility_conditions.source_text: service accrued more than seven (7) years ago shall not be counted toward the minimum requirement.

employee_eligibility_conditions.notes: Must be 12 consecutive months as a full-time employee to be eligible for up to 4 months parental leave. Employees with less than 1 year of service may be granted up to 30 workdays following birth.
employee_eligibility_conditions.source_text: Any employee who has been employed in state service for at least twelve (12) consecutive months as a full-time employee may be absent from employment for a period not to exceed four (4) months for adoption, pregnancy, childbirth, and nursing the infant.

employee_eligibility_conditions.notes: Employees with less than 1 year of service are eligible for parental leave not to exceed 30 workdays following the birth of a child only.
employee_eligibility_conditions.source_text: Any employee who has less than one (1) year of service may be granted parental leave for a period not to exceed thirty (30) workdays following the birth of a child.
